stories.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
stories.description: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

stories.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
stories.description: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

stories.title: As fuel prices continue to rise, Iran war drives Europe toward new crisis
stories.description: As fuel protests bring Ireland to a standstill and UK leaders decry price hikes driven by the Iran war, Europe -- barely recovered from the energy shock of the Ukraine war -- faces another crisis.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: Israeli demolitions levelling towns in south Lebanon, satellite images show
stories.description: BBC Verify analysis found more than 1,400 buildings had been destroyed since 2 March.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US
stories.description: Israel strikes hit southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fires rockets at Israel, a day after rare direct negotiations.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.description: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
stories.description: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Since the start of the US blockade on Monday, 15 vessels have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, nine of which have links to Iran, BBC Verify analysis of ship-tracking data suggests.

stories.title: Turkey orders mass arrests over online praise for recent school shootings
stories.description: Turkish police have ordered the arrest of several dozens of people accused of praising recent school shootings online. At least nine people died in a school shooting in the southern province of Kahramanmaras on Wednesday, and on Tuesday, a former student opened fire at a high sch...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Trump says Israel and Lebanon leaders to hold talks Thursday
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak Thursday, after they held a high-level face-to-face meeting in Washington Tuesday -- the first such negotiation since 1993. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

stories.title: Growing discontent as 36 Democrats back Bernie Sanders’ bid to block arms sales to Israel
stories.description: More than three dozen Democrats backed a bid by Senator Bernie Sanders on Wednesday to block US arms sales to Israel, reflecting growing unease over Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and conflicts in Gaza and Iran. The measures failed, opposed by Republicans, but underscored risi...

stories.title: Jailed Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi in critical condition, supporters say
stories.description: The health of jailed Iranian Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi is critical after she suffered a heart attack last month, supporters said on Wednesday. Her family and lawyers, allowed a second prison visit, reported a sharp deterioration in her condition, with her physic...

stories.title: Israel-Lebanon peace talks: Future negotiations depend on 'confidence-building measures'
stories.description: Angela Diffley is pleased to welcome Bilal Y. Saab, Senior Managing Director of TRENDS Research and Advisory. According to Saab, Lebanon is at a crossroads: the government is committed to reform, is fundamentally “pro-Lebanese” and rejects interference from external actors. The c...

stories.title: Will the Gulf ever be the same? Trump, Iran and the Arabian Peninsula
stories.description: Iran is proving that missiles don't need to travel thousands of kilometres. Across the Gulf is all it takes to force the world's mightiest army to the bargaining table. Pressure on the choke point for one-fifth of the planet's oil and gas is exposing the vulnerability of Arabian ...

stories.title: US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz: What do the numbers show?
stories.description: Between Tehran and Washington's blockades of the Strait of Hormuz, the number of ships passing through the critical waterway has slowed to a trickle. Here's what marine tracking data tells us about the situation.

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports 'fully implemented'
stories.description: The US military said Tuesday that it successfully stopped six ships from sailing out of Iranian ports during the first 24 hours of a naval blockade against the Islamic republic. Central Command (CENTCOM) – which is responsible for American forces in the Middle East – said more th...

stories.title: Why many Kashmiris are donating gold, breaking piggy banks for Iran
stories.description: A centuries-old historical bond has come alive again amid the war on Iran, with Kashmiris sharing whatever they can.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 48 of the US-Iran conflict?
stories.description: US–Iran talks gain pace as Pakistan mediates, optimism for a new round of talks in Islamabad.

stories.title: Israel’s military kills four Lebanese paramedics in consecutive strikes
stories.description: An Israeli triple-tap attack on rescue workers in southern Lebanon has killed four and left several others injured.

stories.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon’s leaders will speak on Thursday
stories.description: The US president says the conversation will be the first between the Israeli and Lebanese leaders in 34 years.

stories.title: Hopes grow for a breakthrough in US-Iran talks as Pakistan mediates
stories.description: Pakistani sources say there has been a 'major breakthrough' over a deal on Iran’s nuclear programme.

stories.title: Democrats clash with US Energy Secretary over Iran war and gas prices
stories.description: Watch the moment a Democratic congresswoman tells the US Energy Secretary he is ‘living in a different world’.

stories.title: Pakistan expecting ‘major breakthrough’ tied to Iran’s nuclear programme
stories.description: A source has told Al Jazeera that Pakistan is expecting a breakthrough tied to Iran’s nuclear programme.

stories.title: Iran war live: Pakistan in push for new round of US-Iran peace negotiations
stories.description: At least 2,167 people have been killed and more than 7,000 injured in Israeli attacks on Lebanon since March 2.

stories.title: Amid Lebanon truce talks, Israel kills 3 paramedics
stories.description: Wael Sabbagh, whose mother Afaf Sidaoui and brother Hassan were killed in an Israeli strike on an apartment building, stands outside the site of the attack in Beirut.—Reuters BEIRUT: Amid efforts of a ceasefire in Lebanon, Israel continued its attacks and targeted paramedic teams...

stories.title: Fuel supply fears after blaze tears through crucial Australian refinery
stories.description: Columns of fire engulfed a crucial Australian oil refinery after a chain of explosions, authorities said on Thursday, as they warned of disruptions to domestic fuel supply. Flames as high as 60 metres erupted late on Wednesday after a gas leak ignited at the Viva fuel plant in Vi...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz concludes visit to Saudi Arabia, departs for Doha: PMO
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has concluded his official visit to Saudi Arabia and departed for Qatar’s capital Doha, the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) said on Thursday. In a statement posted to social media, the PMO said that PM Shehbaz will hold a bilateral meeting in Doha with...

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 crew members of two Iranian warships
stories.description: COLOMBO: Over 200 Iranian crew members from two warships who were rescued by Sri Lanka have been sent back to their homeland, a top official said on Wednesday. Deputy Defence Minister Aruna Jayasekara said 32 sailors rescued from the IRIS Dena — a frigate attacked by US on March ...

stories.title: Nato chief, Kyiv allies vow not to lose sight of Ukraine
stories.description: BERLIN: Nato chief Mark Rutte and Kyiv’s top allies vowed on Wednesday they would not lose sight of Ukraine’s conflict with Russia and called on others to urgently boost their support for the country. With the outbreak of the US-Israeli war against Iran, fears have grown that int...

stories.title: Attacked again by Trump, Pope Leo says world needs message of peace
stories.description: Pope Leo XIV and Cameroon’s First Lady Chantal Biya look on as President Paul Biya delivers a speech in Yaounde.—AFP YAOUNDE: Pope Leo said on Wednesday that the world needs to hear a message of peace and coexistence, after US President Donald Trump attacked him for a second time...

stories.title: Lebanon 'not aware' of any upcoming contact with Israel: official source
stories.description: "We are not aware of any planned contact with the Israeli side, and we have not been informed of any through official channels," the source said

stories.title: Column | Lost in rhetoric
stories.description: How Trump’s Iran narrative reflects a coarsening of global political discourse

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran will not leave Hormuz until its rights are fully secured, says Khamenei's Advisor
stories.description: "Iran will not leave Hormuz until its rights are fully secured": Khamenei's Advisor on US blockade," military adviser of Iran's Supreme Leader, Mohsen Rezaei said

stories.title: Pakistani delegation, led by Army chief Munir, reaches Iran for peace talks
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi welcomed the delegation members upon their arrival in Tehran, according to the Iranian media

stories.title: U.S.-sanctioned supertankers enter Gulf despite blockade
stories.description: Iran’s Fars News Agency said ‌on ⁠Wednesday (April 15, 2026) that an Iranian supertanker subject to U.S. sanctions crossed ⁠the strait towards Iran’s Imam Khomeini port despite the blockade

stories.title: U.S. rules out renewal of exemption from sanctions on Russian, Iranian oil
stories.description: On March 5, the U.S. issued a 30-day sanctions waiver to India, permitting it to buy Russian oil despite sanctions imposed over the Ukraine war

stories.title: Pakistan values Russia’s support for efforts to resolve US-Iran conflict — MFA
stories.description: Pakistani Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Tahir Andrabi added that Russia was "an important global player"

stories.title: Lebanon 'unaware' of any upcoming talks with Israel — AFP
stories.description: According to the report, Beirut has not received information about upcoming contact through official channels

stories.title: Houthi rebels may shut down oil trafficking route through Red Sea — TV
stories.description: According to CNN, such a move would be a blow to the world economy and would surely heap political pressure on Donald Trump

stories.title: Thailand asks Oman to help ensure safe passage of ships via Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Thai Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow also said that Thailand is interested in importing oil, gas, and fertilizers from Oman
